_meta.description: Machine-readable component map. Read before exploring the codebase. Update as part of every feature branch that adds or moves components.
_meta.upstream_debt_legend: Files/dirs still using HestiaCP-specific paths (/usr/local/hestia, /etc/hestiacp, hestia-nginx, hestia-php) that need migration to HestiaRE conventions.
_meta.os_targets: All four are first-class, equal-priority: debian-bookworm (12), debian-trixie (13), ubuntu-noble (24.04), ubuntu-26lts (26.04). load_os_profile (func/helper.sh) is the single source for OS_ID/CODENAME/RELEASE/EXIM_USR/BASE_PKGS_EXTRA. Every feature must work on all four; verify on the VM fleet before a PR.
_meta.distro_quirks: Cross-cutting gotchas that bite more than one service (grep these before assuming a service failure is your bug): (1) Ubuntu 26.04 ships ENFORCED AppArmor profiles that deny access other distros allow — proftpd can't read the panel cert under /usr/local/hestia/ssl (h-add-sys-proftpd drops share/proftpd/apparmor-local into the profile's local/ hook), and mariadbd's profile denies the dac_override the mariadb-install-db bootstrap needs (h-add-sys-mariadb unloads it for the init, reloads after). Suspect AppArmor on any 'Permission denied as root' on ub26. (2) Package names/splits differ by distro+version: proftpd-basic is bookworm-only (else proftpd-core), and modern proftpd (1.3.9 / 1.3.8 on trixie+ubuntu) moved mod_tls into proftpd-mod-crypto — omitting it silently disables FTPS. libzip is libzip4t64 on ub24 (t64 transition) but libzip5 on ub26. (3) apt-cache/apt-get output is localized — always LC_ALL=C before grepping for 'Candidate:' etc. (4) dovecot 2.3 (deb12) vs 2.4 (deb13/ub26) need version-split configs (share/dovecot/{2.3,2.4}/).

install_paths.shell_profile: /etc/profile.d/hestia.sh (0600, root-only — #210; non-root login shells skip it via /etc/profile's [ -r ] test)
install_paths.note: install_root is intentionally identical to HestiaCP — only instance_config changed (/etc/hestiacp → /etc/hestia). See PATHS.md for full reference.

upstream_provenance.purpose: Maps HestiaRE files back to their HestiaCP origin so an upstream advisory or bugfix can be diffed/cherry-picked against the right file. Fork point: HestiaCP 1.9.6, kept read-only in the upstream/hestiacp branch. Each component carries an `upstream_ref` using these tags.

tags.near-verbatim: upstream file, renamed and/or path-migrated (v-*→h-*, /etc/hestiacp→/etc/hestia) but logic intact — cherry-pick candidate for isolated bugfixes
tags.reimplemented: clean HestiaRE reimplementation that diverges from upstream — do NOT cherry-pick, port by hand; the v-* symlink is usually removed (symlink_policy.escalation_rule)
tags.moved: upstream file relocated in-tree (mainly install/deb/* and install/common/* → share/*, #119) but otherwise near-verbatim
tags.removed: upstream component deleted from HestiaRE (bind9/DNS #58/#283, web terminal #59, REST API #146, SpamAssassin #284, software installer #56)
tags.hestiare-native: no upstream equivalent — HestiaRE-only (Caddy + hestia-php panel services, the pure-bash installer, panel Eigenbau libs, repo tooling)

upstream_provenance.cherry_pick_rule: Direct cherry-pick ONLY for verbatim/near-verbatim files carrying an isolated, dependency-free bugfix (CLAUDE.md). reimplemented/moved/native = port manually. Always verify installed==fixed against the actual file, never the version label (#386 method).
upstream_provenance.verification_note: The #386 triage is a worked example: near-verbatim files (func/db.sh) took a direct helper cherry-pick; reimplemented ones (func/main.sh parse_object_kv_list, the search-object commands) were confirmed safe/ported by hand, not cherry-picked.

drift_watch.purpose: Machine-checkable per-file drift markers (#412). share/upstream/sync-upstream.sh reads this map and, after refreshing the upstream/hestiacp snapshot, flags any listed file that upstream MOVED since its checked_ref — so drift is caught deterministically, not by chance (the jailbash 5-bind drift behind `--tmpfs /usr/share` was a lucky find). checked_ref is the upstream/hestiacp commit the HestiaRE copy was last human-verified against; bump it ONLY after re-verifying the file against the newer upstream. upstream_path handles renames (v-*→h-*). A ref-stamp (not a flat ignore list) distinguishes 'deliberately diverged, verified at ref X' from 'not looked at yet'.

install/common/bubblewrap/jailbash.note: byte-identical to upstream at this ref (#412 re-sync of the 5 --tmpfs /usr/share binds)

cli_commands.description: All CLI commands. h-* prefix is HestiaRE-native (renamed from v-* in Issue #22). v-* symlinks for HestiaCP compatibility added in Issue #23. No hl-* commands.

symlink_policy.baseline: Each h-* command that predated Issue #23 has one v-* symlink. New h-* commands created after Issue #23 get NO v-* symlink — the v-* name never existed.
symlink_policy.escalation_rule: When a command is substantially rewritten and diverges significantly from its HestiaCP upstream equivalent, its v-* symlink is removed. Rationale: prevent accidental upstream cherry-picks onto an internally incompatible implementation. Applied in Issue #54 (v-add-sys-roundcube removed after total rewrite for OS-package migration).
symlink_policy.deletion: When removing an h-* command: remove its v-* symlink too — no orphans.
symlink_policy.creation: Committed v-* symlinks ship in the tarball, so the installer does NOT blanket-create them (a `for h-* ; ln -s v-*` loop minted an orphan alias for every HestiaRE-native command, #123). configure_hestia only prunes dangling v-* (a `find -xtype l` — e.g. an alias left pointing at a renamed/removed h-*); h-check-sys-smoke check_v_symlinks_resolve guards against a dangling alias at runtime. No allowlist: an extra working alias for a native command is harmless and not worth maintaining a list for a compat layer meant to fade out.

dns.note: Only h-list-mail-domain-dkim-dns retained — DNS zone management removed in Issue #58

upstream_ref.rule: bin/h-* ← bin/v-* (rename #22/#23; v-* kept as compat symlinks). Default provenance near-verbatim (renamed + /etc/hestiacp→/etc/hestia). Listed key_files:
upstream_ref.bin/h-add-database: bin/v-add-database (near-verbatim; SQL-escapes the password via func/db.sh, #386)
upstream_ref.reimplemented (v-* symlink removed): DB-engine lifecycle h-add/delete-sys-{mariadb,postgresql} + h-upgrade-sys-mariadb (#121/#207); h-add/delete-sys-{roundcube,snappymail} (#54/#234); h-search-user-object + h-search-object (#386 cr7q)
upstream_ref.removal_verb: Single removal verb h-delete-* across all commands (#123, upstream v-delete-* parity). The interim h-remove-sys-* names (redis in #121, etc.) were renamed to h-delete-sys-*.

issues: $HESTIA variable itself is unchanged (/usr/local/hestia) — no migration needed in bin/*
h-add-cron-hestia-autoupdate references HestiaCP update mechanism — remove or replace (see PATHS.md Q5)

func_libraries.description: Shared bash function libraries sourced by all CLI commands and the installer. Core runtime dependency.

file_roles.main.sh: Core utilities: source_conf(), check_result(), output formatting, logging, JSON/shell serialization. Object parsing: parse_object_kv_list (PHP tokenizer, quote-safe, single eval — the upstream double-eval RCE GHSA-w3mx is NOT present, empirically verified #386) and parse_object_kv_list_non_eval (declare -g, no eval — use this for any user-controlled conf-field payload). SFTP jail helpers add_chroot_jail/delete_chroot_jail (systemd bind-mount of the home into /srv/jail). manage_sshd_allowusers add|del <user> (#412): co-maintains the AllowUsers login allowlist in /etc/ssh/sshd_config for panel users — opt-in (acts only if an AllowUsers line exists; installer seeds one commented), edits ONLY the token whose base==user so operator entries (root@IP, maintenance) and the commented/active state survive, validates with `sshd -t` before swapping, reloads sshd only when active, re-comments rather than leaving an active line empty (lockout guard); called from h-add-user/h-delete-user AND rebuild_user (func/rebuild.sh) so restore/rebuild — which bypass h-add-user — re-add the user (else a restored user is missing from an active line and silently locked out).
file_roles.domain.sh: Domain validation and template checking for web and mail
file_roles.rebuild.sh: User and domain rebuild logic — regenerates service configs from HestiaRE data. Queue invariant: lines appended to conf/queue/*.pipe (run via `bash` by h-update-sys-queue) must contain only validated identifiers (username/domain), never free text (GHSA-47mf).
file_roles.syshealth.sh: System health monitoring, RRD graph generation, service status checks
file_roles.db.sh: MySQL/MariaDB and PostgreSQL connection helpers, host configuration. Passwords entering SQL MUST go through mysql_sql_escape()/sql_escape() before 'IDENTIFIED BY'/'PASSWORD' literals (GHSA-8w7m, #386); no eval in this file.
file_roles.backup.sh: Local, FTP, and Restic backup/restore handlers with retention logic
file_roles.ip.sh: IP address validation, RDNS checking, IPv4/IPv6 utilities

upstream_ref.func/main.sh: func/main.sh (near-verbatim; parse_object_kv_list reimplemented — PHP tokenizer replacing the upstream double-eval, GHSA-w3mx #386)
upstream_ref.func/wizard.sh + func/helper.sh: hestiare-native (bash installer, no upstream — see make_release)

issues: $HESTIA variable unchanged (/usr/local/hestia) — no path migration needed in func/*

ssh_shell_jail.description: SSH/SFTP access control: the 3-tier access ladder — nologin (SFTP-only chroot, the default) · jailbash (bubblewrap-sandboxed interactive shell, opt-in) · bash (unconfined). #223 was the baseline check; the non-SFTP hardening landed in #412 (Story 1). The SFTP transport/chroot rebuild (drop /srv/jail bind-mounts, domain-FTP sub-accounts) is deferred to Story 2 — see docs branch jail-chroot-story{1,2}-plan.md.
ssh_shell_jail.jailbash: install/common/bubblewrap/jailbash — bwrap wrapper run as the jailbash login shell: reuses host /usr read-only but with private /tmp,/home,/var,/run and stub passwd/group, --unshare-all (incl. pid → fresh /proc) --share-net. NEAR-VERBATIM UPSTREAM (origin/upstream/hestiacp) — tracked for drift (the #412 5-bind re-sync of zoneinfo/php/ca-certificates/i18n + /etc/php, lost behind `--tmpfs /usr/share`, was a chance find; a CI drift-gate is planned). Registered as a login shell in /etc/shells by h-add-sys-ssh-jail. NOTE: --share-net means 127.0.0.1 inside the jail is the HOST loopback (a jailbash user reaches host-bound services: redis:6379, webmail FPM :8090/:8091, panel, adminer) — an open isolation question tracked in Story 1 verification, not yet closed.
ssh_shell_jail.apparmor_userns_quirk: On Ubuntu 24.04+/kernels with apparmor_restrict_unprivileged_userns=1, unprivileged user namespaces are blocked and bwrap fails without the `bwrap-userns-restrict` AppArmor profile. That profile is installed ONLY when /etc/apparmor.d/abi/4.0 exists (the artefact-based guard, NOT an OS-version flag). Keep this rationale verbatim — hard-won.
ssh_shell_jail.shell_allowlist: func/main.sh HESTIA_SHELL_ALLOWLIST + list_allowed_shells (#412): the single curated source (nologin/jailbash/bash/sh ∩ /etc/shells, nologin guaranteed) shared by is_format_valid_shell (hard validator) AND h-list-sys-shells (the panel dropdown source for web/edit/user + web/{add,edit}/package). Dropped from upstream: rssh (gone from Debian, silently degrades to nologin), screen/tmux (useless as login shell), dash/rbash. The validator is genuinely hard (not a UI-only filter): h-change-user-shell gates every genuine change through it, EXCEPT re-asserting the user's current shell (in-allowlist OR ==current — so a legacy off-list shell a restore left in place can be re-set, but no new off-list shell slips in). rebuild.sh sets the shell straight from /etc/shells (bypasses the validator) so restore stays tolerant; the user/package editors render an existing off-allowlist shell as a selected "(current)" option so an unchanged save never resets it. FTP_SHELL (domain-FTP sub-accounts, h-add-web-domain-ftp/rebuild.sh) is a SECOND shell source that bypasses both panel and validator → Story 2.
ssh_shell_jail.allowusers: func/main.sh manage_sshd_allowusers add|del <user> (#412) — co-maintains an AllowUsers login allowlist in /etc/ssh/sshd_config for panel users; installer seeds it commented/inert. See func_libraries.file_roles.main.sh for the full contract. Domain-FTP sub-accounts → Story 2.
ssh_shell_jail.srv_jail: add_chroot_jail/delete_chroot_jail (func/main.sh) build a per-user systemd bind-mount of the real home into /srv/jail/$user for the SFTP ChrootDirectory (h-add-sys-sftp-jail: `Match User` + ChrootDirectory /srv/jail/%u + internal-sftp). The bind is cosmetic PATH FIDELITY (same inodes) — the permission mechanism is the root-ownership flip of /home/$user. This whole /srv/jail machinery + the growing `Match User` list are what Story 2 replaces (pam_namespace primary candidate).

web_panel.description: Plain-PHP control panel UI. No framework. Action-based routing via directory structure. Served by Caddy + PHP 8.3 FPM (hestia-php.service) on port 8083.

panel_vendor.strategy: No Composer at install time. No vendor/ in repo. OS packages and Eigenbau only.

os_packages.libphp-phpmailer: deb12=6.6.3 / ub24=6.8.1 / deb13=6.9.3 — panel email sending — LGPL-2.1-only — installed by install_panel stage

inline_libs.web/inc/lib/quoteshellarg.php: Third-party: inlined from hestiacp/phpquoteshellarg v1.1.0 (Unlicense). Keeps namespace Hestiacp\quoteshellarg intact. See VENDORED.json and security note in file header.
inline_libs.web/inc/lib/totp.php: Eigenbau: RFC 6238 TOTP (secret gen, URI builder, verify). No upstream, no deps.
inline_libs.web/inc/lib/qrcode.php: Eigenbau: single-file SVG QR code generator (byte mode, ECC-M, v1-10). No upstream, no deps.
inline_libs.web/inc/cloudflare-ip.php: Eigenbau: static Cloudflare CIDR list. Update from cloudflare.com/ips-v4 and /ips-v6 when ranges change.

removed: robthree/twofactorauth — removed in Issue #56, replaced by web/inc/lib/totp.php (Eigenbau)
phpmailer/phpmailer — removed in Issue #56, replaced by OS package libphp-phpmailer (install_panel stage)
chillerlan/php-qrcode + php-settings-container — removed in Issue #56, replaced by web/inc/lib/qrcode.php (Eigenbau)

pma_sso.description: phpMyAdmin SSO without REST API (#145): web/sso/phpmyadmin/ runs h-add-database-sso-token via panel sudo (temp DB user + one-time handoff file /run/hestia-sso/<token>, root:caddy 0730/640); share/phpmyadmin/hestia-sso.php (PMA pool, unprivileged) consumes the token and opens the signon session. Enable/disable: h-add-sys-pma-sso / h-delete-sys-pma-sso; PHPMYADMIN_KEY is only the enabled flag.

upstream_ref.rule: web/* ← web/* (near-verbatim + rebrand); the panel is inherited. Notable divergences:
upstream_ref.web/inc/helpers.php: web/inc/helpers.php (near-verbatim; get_real_user_ip trusts CF-Connecting-IP only behind CF ranges, GHSA-73p3)
upstream_ref.web/edit/server/hestia/index.php: web/edit/server/hestiaweb/index.php (near-verbatim; hestiaweb→hestia #214; GHSA-fcq6 admin gate fixed #386)

issues: PHP files reference /usr/local/hestia paths in includes — install root is unchanged so paths are correct, but hardcoded (not using a constant)
web/inc/mail-wrapper.php shebang: updated to #!/usr/bin/php8.3 in Issue #25
Panel branding still references HestiaCP in some locale strings

panel_assets.description: Panel frontend assets, served as-is by Panel-Caddy — no build step, no Node/npm (#248). JS: native ES modules from web/js/src/ (explicit .js import extensions). CSS: native nesting, plain min-width media queries, browser resolves @import chains. Browser baseline: adr-browser-baseline.md (Chrome/Edge 120, Firefox 117, Safari 17.2) with CSS-only warning banner in web/templates/header.php.
panel_assets.themes: Shipped: web/css/src/themes/*.css — light is the base stylesheet (css.php always loads it), overlays: dark, dark-tonal (@import dark.css + tonal green icon ladder, #260/#269), light-flat, light-green (dark green primary via brand-token redefinition, #269). Renames #269: default→light, flat→light-flat; vestia removed #259 — THEME migration + stale-file cleanup in func/helper.sh migrate_data_layout. Brand tokens --brand-* (green/anthracite, #259) in light.css :root, dark.css overrides greens brighter. Custom: web/css/src/themes/custom/*.css (instance data, gitignored). Listed by bin/h-list-sys-themes via HESTIA_THEMES/HESTIA_THEMES_CUSTOM (func/main.sh).
panel_assets.vendored: web/js/vendor/ (Alpine.js + collapse), web/css/vendor/ (normalize.css, Font Awesome Free solid-only). Manifest: VENDORED.json; snapshot branches: upstream/alpinejs, upstream/normalize-css, upstream/fontawesome. Updater: share/upstream/update-web-vendor.sh (--check read-only version report vs VENDORED.json; --fetch <asset>[@version] rebuilds one snapshot branch, publisher-hash-verified); hooked into share/upstream/sync-upstream.sh (author's sync instance).
panel_assets.upstream_ref: reimplemented/native: the Node/npm build step was removed (#248), so web/js/src + web/css/src are HestiaRE-native (native ES modules / native CSS, not upstream's built assets). web/{js,css}/vendor/ are upstream third-party (Alpine.js, normalize.css, Font Awesome Free) tracked in VENDORED.json + upstream/{alpinejs,normalize-css,fontawesome} snapshot branches — NOT from hestiacp; refresh via share/upstream/update-web-vendor.sh.

installer.description: Service config templates and data distributed during install. Consumed by Makefile install-* targets. hst-install-debian.sh and hst-install-ubuntu.sh removed in Issue #26b — replaced by Makefile targets.
installer.note: Monolithic bash installers removed in Issue #26b. install/hst-install.sh removed in Issue #26d. Install logic: Makefile (removed) → Justfile #102 → pure-bash bin/h-install-hestia + func/wizard.sh + func/helper.sh in Issue #112 (just removed). See make_release component.

upstream_ref.share/php-fpm/: install/deb/php-fpm/ (moved #119/#397; shared www.conf pool removed #397 — per-domain pools only, apache catch-all now Require-all-denied)
upstream_ref.share/{apache2,nginx,dovecot,hestia}/log: install/deb/logrotate/ (moved #119; distributed per-service, dir dissolved — cf. share/roundcube/logrotate #234)
upstream_ref.share/{nginx,apache2}/webmail/: install/deb/templates/mail/ (reworked to Panel-Caddy proxies #205; moved per-service #119, MAILTPL retired; dead rainloop removed)
upstream_ref.share/proftpd/: install/deb/proftpd/ (moved #123; deployed by h-add-sys-proftpd — was orphaned; +Include modules.conf/conf.d; +apparmor-local for Ubuntu 26's enforced proftpd profile). h-add-sys-proftpd now sets FTP_SYSTEM=proftpd (never set before, so h-restart-ftp / RRD FTP graph / smoke FTP check / the NAT MasqueradeAddress branch were all inert on pre-#123 installs).
upstream_ref.share/clamav/: install/deb/clamav/clamd.conf moved here (#123; deployed by h-add-sys-clamav — was orphaned; hardened LocalSocketMode 666→660 + LogVerbose off) plus two NEW curated files: socket-hardening.conf (systemd .socket drop-in) and apparmor-local. ClamAV is the antivirus analogue of rspamd: the exim `.ifdef CLAMD` block (av_scanner=clamd: + per-domain antivirus ACL + `deny malware=*/defer_ok`) was fully shipped but inert (macro commented). h-add-sys-clamav sets ANTIVIRUS_SYSTEM=clamav and arms the CLAMD macro ONLY after clamd answers on the socket (clamdscan --ping) — DB-download timeout leaves it OFF + honest WARN, because `defer_ok` is FAIL-OPEN (dead clamd → mail accepted UNSCANNED, not deferred), so an armed-but-blind macro would silently pass mail. THREE non-obvious wiring facts, all found in live testing: (1) BIDIRECTIONAL group membership — exim joins clamav (write the socket) AND clamav joins Debian-exim (READ /var/spool/exim4/scan, 0750 exim:exim); the second is load-bearing, without it clamd errors 'Permission denied' on the spool → fail-open passes mail unscanned. (2) The 660 socket is enforced by socket-hardening.conf (SocketMode=0660), NOT clamd.conf's LocalSocketMode — clamd is socket-activated so the clamav-daemon.socket unit owns the live /run/clamav/clamd.ctl mode (default 0666); the add script installs the drop-in + `systemctl restart clamav-daemon.socket`, and restarts the daemon (not enable --now) so it re-forks with the new group + socket. (3) apparmor-local re-asserts `/var/spool/exim4/** r,` into the clamd profile's local hook — the stock usr.sbin.clamd profile already grants it (0 denials on all 4 distros), this is defensive for a stricter base install; guarded on the profile file + `apparmor_parser … || true`, so a no-AppArmor host installs fine and the exim chain still scans (it relies on unix group perms, not AppArmor). Delete = saved-state (per-domain flags kept, restored on reinstall); the virus DB is moved aside across the purge and restored (apt purge clamav-freshclam wipes /var/lib/clamav) unless PURGE_DATA=yes, and the add script chowns it back to clamav in case the purge/reinstall re-allocated the uid. list_mail.php AV/spam columns now gate on ANTIVIRUS_SYSTEM/ANTISPAM_SYSTEM (neutral dash when the addon is gone).

panel_caddy_service.description: Caddy config for the HestiaRE admin panel on port 8083. Replaces the compiled hestia-nginx package. Handles PHP via FastCGI and X-Accel-Redirect for file downloads.
panel_caddy_service.notes_webmail: webmail-*.conf: internal loopback listeners (127.0.0.1:8090/8091) that render Roundcube/SnappyMail via dedicated caddy FPM pools (share/panel-php/pool.d/{roundcube,snappymail}.conf); deployed by h-add-sys-{roundcube,snappymail}. Per-domain webmail.<domain> vhosts reverse-proxy to these — share/{nginx,apache2}/webmail/{default,web_system,snappymail,default_snappymail}.{tpl,stpl} are docroot-free proxies (proxy_pass/ProxyPass to :8090 rc / :8091 sm; moved from templates/mail per-service #119, resolver $HESTIA/share/$WEB_SYSTEM/webmail/$tpl, MAILTPL retired); {disabled,default_disabled} are the backend-safe fallback (local web stack / 404, no dead proxy). LE http-01 served locally (nginx inline return-200 include; apache-only .well-known alias + ProxyPass exclusion + AllowOverride None on the docroot, needs mod_proxy_http). Rendered by func/domain.sh add_webmail_config; template chosen by func/domain.sh select_webmail_template (shared by h-add-mail-domain-webmail + h-add-mail-domain-ssl, degrades an uninstalled/empty client to disabled — #119) (#205 part 2). h-add/remove-sys-{roundcube,snappymail} re-render all mail domains (h-update-mail-templates) so install/removal takes effect immediately. apps/webmail.tpl -> /etc/caddy/apps/webmail.conf: handle_path /webmail/* route INSIDE the :8083 panel site (phpMyAdmin/Adminer model) sharing the Roundcube pool, so Roundcube is also reachable at :8083/webmail for admin access; Roundcube-only (SnappyMail is root-mounted, no sub-path). Deployed/removed by h-add/remove-sys-roundcube (#205 part 1b).
notes: X-Accel-Redirect handled via handle_response in reverse_proxy block
web/list/rrd/image.php updated to send absolute path for X-Accel-Redirect
File manager /fm/* served via handle block with uri strip_prefix
Web terminal removed in Issue #59 — /_shell/ location absent by design

Panel apps under /etc/caddy/apps/*.conf: phpMyAdmin/phpPgAdmin (h-change-sys-db-alias) and rspamd controller UI (installer mail stage, Issue #301). rspamd: /rspamd/* is forward_auth-gated against the admin panel session (web/rspamd-auth.php) then proxied to the controller's unix socket /run/rspamd/controller.sock (X-Forwarded-* stripped). The controller binds that socket (mode 0660, group _rspamd) instead of TCP localhost so no local shell user can read it; installer adds caddy to the _rspamd group. Shown in-panel via the iframe page web/list/rspamd/. Installer also sets password+enable_password overriding the stock q1 default (defense in depth)
panel_caddy_service.upstream_ref: hestiare-native — replaces the compiled hestia-nginx package; no upstream equivalent (#24). Roundcube panel route + internal webmail listeners are HestiaRE-only (#205).

panel_php_service.description: Dedicated PHP-FPM service for the HestiaRE panel. Runs as hestia-php.service using Sury php8.3-fpm binary with an isolated pool. Replaces compiled hestia-php package.
panel_php_service.confd_isolation: The panel FPM runs with PHP_INI_SCAN_DIR=/etc/php/hestia/fpm/conf.d (set in hestia-php-fpm) so it loads a curated extension set independent of the customer version's conf.d (#272). hestia-php-confd rebuilds it from the pinned version's mods-available; called by install_panel, h-change-sys-panel-php, and migrate_data_layout. Wrapper falls back to the default scan dir if unbuilt. APP→EXTENSION COUPLING (the reason the set is what it is): EVERY app on this single FPM master shares this one fpm conf.d — (1) panel UI web/, (2) phpMyAdmin+Adminer, (3) webmail Roundcube+SnappyMail (#205). hestia-php-confd curates them as fpm_* / fpm_apps_* / fpm_webmail_* respectively. AUDIT RULE: before dropping any extension grep all three app groups, not just the panel/DB tools — a 2026-07 audit dropped `dom` after checking only panel+phpMyAdmin+Adminer and 500'd every Roundcube page (Roundcube's html.php renders via DOMDocument). Non-obvious members: intl (webmail IDN), phar (SnappyMail plugins), dom (Roundcube). Excluded on purpose: imagick/imap/ldap/soap/bcmath/apcu (customer-app extensions). sodium is compiled-in on Sury (no mods-available .ini).
panel_php_service.version_indirection: The panel PHP version is the single source of truth in /etc/php/hestia/php-version (written by h-install-hestia, read by the hestia-php / hestia-php-fpm wrappers). h-change-sys-panel-php (#272) rewrites it + restarts hestia-php; h-delete-web-php refuses to remove the pinned version. Distinct from h-change-sys-php (default web PHP).
panel_php_service.note: gettext included in php8.3-common (auto dep of php8.3-fpm). Installer integration completed in Issue #26a.
panel_php_service.upstream_ref: hestiare-native — replaces the compiled hestia-php package with an isolated Sury FPM pool; no upstream equivalent (#25/#272).

make_release.description: Pure-bash installer. install.sh (bootstrap) → func/wizard.sh (manifest-driven Q&A → /etc/hestia/install.conf) → bin/h-install-hestia (non-interactive, COMPONENT_*-gated). Migrated from Justfile/just/* to bash h-* commands in Issue #112 (epic); just removed (was Makefile→Justfile in #102, Justfile→bash in #112).

structure.install.sh: Bootstrap (curl-able): root + OS detect, apt prereqs (curl jq whiptail gnupg ca-certificates — no just), --dev source setup, fetch+extract release tarball, run func/wizard.sh, seed_hestia_etc, hand off to bin/h-install-hestia.
structure.func/wizard.sh: Interactive whiptail wizard (bash fallback), manifest-driven: pre-questions + preset + dynamic version discovery + component questions; writes /etc/hestia/install.conf. Runnable standalone (hestia configure).
structure.func/helper.sh: hestia_apt (spinner + apt log split), load_os_profile (OS_ID/CODENAME/RELEASE/EXIM_USR/BASE_PKGS_EXTRA for deb12/deb13/ub24/ub26), seed_hestia_etc (hestia.env + profile.d + seed hestia.conf).
structure.bin/h-install-hestia: Non-interactive orchestrator: validates /etc/hestia, sources hestia.env+main.sh+helper.sh+install.conf, runs stages gated by COMPONENT_*. Idempotent via .done.* sentinels (exit→return).
structure.bin/hestia: Umbrella dispatcher: hestia install|configure|update|uninstall|status (no v-* alias).
structure.bin/h-update-hestia: Update whole distribution from release tarball (+ --check); stops/starts hestia around copy. Does NOT use the legacy h-update-sys-hestia* apt/git updaters (removal tracked in #128).
structure.bin/h-uninstall-hestia: Conservative uninstall (keeps /home + DBs always; --purge removes packages + /usr/local/hestia + /etc/hestia).
structure.bin/h-list-sys-install: Status report: version, source, OS, profile, .done.* phases, COMPONENT_*.

stages.install_base: APT repos (Sury always; MariaDB only for an external-repo version), base packages, system users, SSH hardening (Subsystem internal-sftp, LoginGraceTime 1m, DebianBanner no, and a commented #AllowUsers allowlist seed co-maintained by manage_sshd_allowusers, #412), NTP, /proc
stages.install_web: nginx + apache2 per WEB_SERVER with port model #247 (BOTH: nginx proxy 80/443 -> apache2 8080/8443; APACHE/NGINX alone: 80/443; Listen per IP via h-add-sys-ip, ports.conf emptied, mod_remoteip for client IP behind proxy), multi-PHP via COMPONENT_PHP_MULTIPHP_VERSIONS when sury_multi, web pool
stages.install_db: MariaDB (version from COMPONENT_DB_MARIADB_VERSION); phpMyAdmin/PostgreSQL/Redis per components
stages.install_mail: exim4, dovecot (version-aware), rspamd (optional), webmailer roundcube/snappymail — only if COMPONENT_MAIL_BLOCK_PRESENT
stages.install_addons: proftpd (→ h-add-sys-proftpd) / docker / filemanager per components (#123)
stages.configure_hestia: sudoers, v-* dangling-symlink prune, SSL cert, admin user, firewall, default domain, crontab; writes .done.configure
stages.install_tools: always-installed utilities + selected TOOLS_SELECTION + composer + wp-cli
stages.finalize_install: start hestia.service, schedule LE host cert, final apt upgrade, print credentials

make_release.sentinel_files: /etc/hestia/.done.{base,panel,web,db,mail,security,addons,configure} — presence skips that stage (return 0, NOT exit)
make_release.presets: 7 manifest presets (standard/compact/latest/singlephp/nomail/mailonly/custom) drive COMPONENT_* in the wizard; h-install-hestia consumes COMPONENT_* directly (no profile-name dispatch)
make_release.bootstrap_flow: curl | bash install.sh → OS detect → apt prereqs → download+extract tarball → func/wizard.sh → seed_hestia_etc → bin/h-install-hestia
make_release.install_conf: /etc/hestia/install.conf — written by func/wizard.sh; HESTIA_* params + COMPONENT_* selections; sourced by h-install-hestia; COMPONENT_* kept current by h-add-*/h-delete-* via set_install_component
make_release.upstream_ref: hestiare-native — the whole installer (install.sh, func/wizard.sh, bin/h-install-hestia, func/helper.sh) was rebuilt from scratch (Makefile→just→bash, #106/#112/#114); upstream's install/hst-install-*.sh were removed (#26). No cherry-pick path.

pr_api.description: Repo API integration for PR creation. The remote host, API call and use of TOKEN live in CLAUDE.local.md (untracked — kept off the public GitHub mirror), NOT here.

minimal_profile: standard minus apache2 and mail stack

panel_php.reference_version_source: share/manifest.json -> components.PANEL_PHP.reference_version (#272): single source for installer PHP_VER + wizard checklist lock; always routed through h-add-web-php for the full extension set

upstream_debt_summary.note: 571 total flagged files; 563 had /etc/hestiacp references migrated to /etc/hestia in Issue #30; src/ directory and install/hst-install.sh deleted in Issue #26d — build_tools component removed. 9 web/ files remain — /usr/local/hestia refs are structurally correct (install root unchanged) except mail-wrapper shebang (deferred Issue #25)

primary_issues.detail: bin/* and func/* sourced /etc/hestiacp/hestia.conf — migrated to /etc/hestia/hestia.conf in Issue #30, renamed to /etc/hestia/hestia.env in Issue #81
Install root stays /usr/local/hestia — $HESTIA is already correct in all bin/* files
hestia-nginx/php/web-terminal removed; Caddy+hestia-php.service added; bind/vsftpd/spamd removed; upgrade/versions/ deleted — completed Issue #26a
